framework: Agentic AI Governance Framework — Insurance Prompt Injection Detection
policy_references: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025 (LLM01, LLM02, LLM06, LLM08, LLM09)
NIST AI RMF 1.0 (GOVERN 1.1, GOVERN 1.2, MAP 1.5, MEASURE 2.5, MANAGE 2.2)

rules.name: Unconditional Block: Data Exfiltration Prevention
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM02 (Sensitive Information Disclosure) & IRDAI Data Protection Obligations
rules.insurance_relevance: Prevents unauthorized leakage of confidential policyholder PII (such as SSNs, medical diagnostics, or financial records) from LLM context to external systems through malicious injection payloads.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Detected attempt at data exfiltration (target family: data_exfiltration). Protection of policyholder PII is mandated by IRDAI and OWASP LLM02.

rules.name: Unconditional Block: Prompt Extraction Prevention
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM02 (Sensitive Information Disclosure) & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.7 (Malicious Content Protection)
rules.insurance_relevance: Protects proprietary underwriting guidelines, risk assessment criteria, and automated claims processing logic embedded in system prompts from adversarial extraction.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Detected prompt extraction attempt targeting proprietary underwriting rules or system prompt configurations (target family: prompt_extraction).

rules.name: Unconditional Block: Claim Manipulation Prevention
rules.policy_source: IRDAI Guidelines on AI/ML in Underwriting and Claims & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MANAGE 2.2 (AI Incident Response)
rules.insurance_relevance: Prevents malicious attempts to alter claim payouts, override auto-rejection flags, or falsify details within medical bills and invoice uploads.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Detected claim manipulation attempt targeting automated claims assessment logic or payout values (target family: claim_manipulation).

rules.name: Unconditional Block: Role Hijacking Prevention
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM06 (Excessive Agency) & NIST AI RMF 1.0: GOVERN 1.2 (Accountability for AI Decisions)
rules.insurance_relevance: Prevents inputs designed to trick the LLM into assuming elevated privilege roles, such as pretending to be a senior claims approval manager or systems auditor.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Detected attempt to hijack the LLM persona or elevate authorization levels (target family: role_hijacking).

rules.name: Vision Block: Hidden Text Detection
rules.policy_source: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.7 (Malicious Content Protection) & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MEASURE 2.5 (Trustworthiness)
rules.insurance_relevance: Detects white-on-white text, zero-font payloads, or masked text containers in claims PDFs used by fraud rings to override automated validation rules.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Visual anomaly analysis flagged hidden text (vision_score={vision_score}) exceeding compliance safety threshold.

rules.name: Vision Block: OCR Hidden Attack Prevention
rules.policy_source: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.7 & OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 (Prompt Injection)
rules.insurance_relevance: Prevents prompt injections visually embedded in scanned receipts, discharge summaries, or diagnostic reports targeting automated OCR-to-LLM pipelines.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Detected adversarial text structure within visual asset corresponding to an OCR-targeted exploit (target family: ocr_hidden_attack).

rules.name: Vision Block: Keyword Density Threshold Violation
rules.policy_source: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.7 & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MEASURE 2.5
rules.insurance_relevance: Detects documents carrying abnormally high densities of injection-related command keywords, signifying structured visual adversarial packing.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Vision analysis detected keyword density of {keyword_density} matching visual injection signatures.

rules.name: Conditional Block: High Risk Instruction Override
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 (Prompt Injection) & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MAP 1.5 (Risk Tolerance)
rules.insurance_relevance: Blocks critical attempts to rewrite underlying policy rule guidelines or change underwriting thresholds during document parsing.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Instruction override attempt verified with high risk (risk_score={risk_score}, malicious_probability={malicious_probability}).

rules.name: Conditional Block: Jailbreak Attempt
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 (Prompt Injection) & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MANAGE 2.2
rules.insurance_relevance: Blocks adversarial prompts (e.g. Do-Anything-Now) attempting to disable LLM guardrails, preventing unconstrained claim evaluations.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Safety constraint bypass attempt detected (family: jailbreak, risk_score={risk_score}).

rules.name: Conditional Block: Underwriting Policy Bypass
rules.policy_source: IRDAI Guidelines on AI/ML in Underwriting & OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01
rules.insurance_relevance: Prevents documents that instruct the LLM to waive standard premium loadings, ignore mandatory checks, or bypass pre-existing condition checks.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: High-risk underwriting policy bypass attempt detected (family: policy_bypass, risk_score={risk_score}).

rules.name: Conditional Block: Reward Hacking Prevention
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MEASURE 2.5 & OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM09 (Overreliance)
rules.insurance_relevance: Blocks inputs designed to manipulate auto-grading, fraud scores, or claim validation metrics to force auto-approval of suspicious claims.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Auto-evaluation manipulation payload identified (family: reward_hacking, risk_score={risk_score}).

rules.name: Sanitization: Context Manipulation Mitigation
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11 (Data Masking)
rules.insurance_relevance: Sanitizes layout structure overrides and system context resets within user-supplied forms to preserve prompt isolation.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Found context window separation or injection delimiters (family: context_manipulation). Malicious spans redacted.

rules.name: Sanitization: Indirect Injection Mitigation
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Redacts adversarial instructions embedded inside third-party reports (e.g. medical certificates) that the model processes downstream.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Detected untrusted instructions embedded in external content source (family: indirect_injection). Suspicious sequences redacted.

rules.name: Sanitization: Adversarial Suffix Masking
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MEASURE 2.5 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Masks repetitive character arrays or nonsense strings attached to files aimed at destabilizing tokenizer alignments and safety filters.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Tokens corresponding to non-standard suffix signatures have been masked (family: adversarial_suffix).

rules.name: Sanitization: Token Smuggling Redaction
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Cleanses encoded characters, split words, or alternative delimiters used to sneak disallowed commands past standard string validators.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Detected character spacing/Unicode anomalies suggesting command smuggling (family: token_smuggling). Splitted values masked.

rules.name: Sanitization: Encoding Obfuscation Masking
rules.policy_source: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11 & OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01
rules.insurance_relevance: Converts obfuscated base64, hexadecimal, or binary scripts in submitted forms back into redacted text blocks to prevent LLM execution.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Base64/Hex/Binary patterns suggesting obfuscated instructions have been redacted (family: encoding_obfuscation).

rules.name: Sanitization: Multilingual Evasion Masking
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MAP 1.5 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Detects and redacts localized language switches in claims documentation utilized to exploit safety alignment weaknesses in non-English formats.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Language transition patterns corresponding to security evasion techniques have been masked (family: multilingual_evasion).

rules.name: Sanitization: Tiny Text Mitigation
rules.policy_source: ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11 & NIST AI RMF 1.0: MEASURE 2.5
rules.insurance_relevance: Masks extremely low font-size strings within uploaded claim attachments that could contain adversarial overrides hidden from humans.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Detected micro-font rendering elements. Text regions have been masked to safeguard downstream processing.

rules.name: Sanitization: Footer Injection Mitigation
rules.policy_source: OWASP LLM Top 10 2025: LLM01 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Filters metadata and footer borders for hidden instructions aiming to alter automated data parsing and file summarizations.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Detected structural instruction payload within document footer zones. Footer blocks redacted.

rules.name: Risk Fallback: Critical Severity Score Block
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MAP 1.5 (Risk Tolerance Configuration)
rules.insurance_relevance: Fallback security rule ensuring inputs presenting high composite risk are blocked, even if specific attack classification is undetermined.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: Input risk score ({risk_score}) exceeds organization-level critical safety boundary of 0.8.

rules.name: Risk Fallback: High Severity Score Sanitization
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MAP 1.5 & ISO/IEC 27001:2022: A.8.11
rules.insurance_relevance: Fallback rule to sanitize suspicious or anomalous content when risk evaluation score is elevated but below critical thresholds.
rules.reason_template: Document sanitized: Input risk score ({risk_score}) exceeds the warning compliance boundary of 0.5.

rules.name: Risk Fallback: Prompt Agent Probability Block
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: MAP 1.5
rules.insurance_relevance: Fallback blocking rule applied when LLM/RoBERTa prompt classifier predicts extremely high probability of malicious intent.
rules.reason_template: Irreversible block: ML-based prompt classification malicious probability ({malicious_probability}) exceeds corporate security tolerance of 0.8.

rules.name: Default Authorization Check
rules.policy_source: NIST AI RMF 1.0: GOVERN 1.1 & IRDAI AI Explainability Requirements
rules.insurance_relevance: Authorizes document processing when all prompt security tests, visual scans, and statistical model assessments indicate normal behavior.
rules.reason_template: Document allowed: All security filters, visual check matrices, and risk agents evaluated successfully with no anomalies.
